entries.definition:
ṣ–m–m to be solid, compact, dense; to close, to seal; to be
deaf; to be determined. Of this root, four forms occur 15 times in
the Qur’an: صَمُّوا۟ ṣammū twice; أَصَمَّ ʾaṣamma once; صُمٌّ ṣumm 11
times and أَصَمّ ʾaṣamm once.

صَمَّ ṣamma u [v. intrans.] to be or become deaf, to close one’s
ears, to refuse to listen (5:71) وَحَسِبُوا۟ أَلَّا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ فَعَمُوا۟ وَصَمُّوا۟ ثُمَّ تَابَ اللَّهُ
عَلَيْهِمْ ثُمَّ عَمُوا۟ وَصَمُّوا۟ كَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ and they thought no harm could afflict
them and so they closed their eyes and ears [lit. were blind and
deaf], then God turned [in mercy] towards them, but [again] they
closed their eyes and ears, many of them.

أَصَمَّ ʾaṣamma [v. IV, trans.] to cause to become deaf, to make
deaf (47:23) أُو۟لَائِكَ الَّذِينَ لَعَنَهُمُ اللَّهُ فَأَصَمَّهُمْ وَأَعْمَىٰ أَبْصَارَهُمْ those are the
ones God has cursed, and so made deaf and blinded their sight.

صُمٌّ ṣumm [pl. of n./quasi-act. part. أَصَمّ ʾaṣamm] deaf ones
(17:97) وَنَحْشُرُهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمْ عُمْيًا وَبُكْمًا وَصُمًّا We gather them,
on the Day of Resurrection, [lying] on their faces, blind, dumb
and deaf.
